title: carex acuta subsp. mauritanica
intro: Carex acuta subsp. mauritanica is a subspecies of sedge, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of essential oils and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Carex acuta subsp. mauritanica include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions that are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
